topic: An athlete doesn't make the team they expected to join
raw_output:
[story 1]

The roar of the crowd was a physical thing, vibrating up through my cleats and settling deep in my chest. I slapped my teammate on the back, a wide, unrestrained grin stretching across my face. We had done it. After all those grueling practices, all those early mornings fueled by lukewarm coffee and sheer stubbornness, we were on the roster. I felt a lightness in my step, a buoyant energy that made my limbs feel impossibly long. The weight of expectation, which had been a heavy cloak moments before, had lifted entirely. I looked at the team photo on the wall, and a genuine, unburdened smile spread across my lips. This was everything.

[story 2]

I practically bounced on the balls of my feet as I walked out of the locker room, my shoulders practically vibrating with excitement. My heart hammered a frantic, happy rhythm against my ribs. I couldn't stop the wide, open smile that pulled at my face, a genuine expression of pure elation. "We made it!" I shouted, my voice cracking slightly with the sheer force of my feeling. The air felt electric, charged with the promise of victory. I felt a rush of warmth flood my core, a sensation so potent it made me want to run in place. Everything aligned perfectly, just as I had envisioned.

[story 3]

I couldn't stop the high-pitched laugh that escaped me as the coach finally announced the final roster. My hands clenched and unclenched at my sides, but the tension wasn't anxiety—it was pure, unadulterated elation. I let out a breath I hadn't realized I was holding, a deep, satisfied sigh. A slow, triumphant smile spread across my face, crinkling the corners of my eyes. I looked around at my teammates, their faces mirroring my own sudden surge of joy. A feeling of absolute rightness washed over me, a powerful sense that this was the culmination of everything.
